creatives.headline: Not all therapy is CBT. And not all conditions respond to CBT.
creatives.caption:
A JAMA meta-analysis of 375 trials found that therapy outcomes vary by 4x depending on the match between condition and modality.

PTSD responds differently than depression. Bipolar responds differently than anxiety. One approach does not fit all.

Manhattan Mental Health Counseling offers CBT, DBT, EMDR, psychodynamic, couples therapy, and more. 80+ therapists. Online across New York State. Insurance accepted.

creatives.headline: 1 in 4 people in trauma therapy drop out. Here's what keeps the other 3.
creatives.caption:
PTSD has the highest therapy efficacy of any mental health condition. It also has the highest dropout rate: 24%.

The difference between someone who stays and someone who leaves often comes down to one thing: the right therapist.

At Manhattan Mental Health Counseling, our trauma specialists are trained in EMDR, CPT, and prolonged exposure. We don't just assign you a therapist. We match you with someone who specializes in what you're going through.

80+ therapists. Online across New York State. Insurance accepted.
creatives.source: Unique to MMHC (no competitor cites research)
creatives.subtext: Therapist expertise matters more than the therapy itself.
creatives.first_comment: If you've tried therapy before and it didn't work, the therapy might not have been the problem. Link in bio.

creatives.headline: DBT reduces suicide attempts by 68% in bipolar disorder. Most practices don't offer DBT.
creatives.caption:
A 2024 JAMA study found that DBT reduces suicide attempts by 68% in people with bipolar disorder. It outperformed CBT significantly for this population.

But most therapy practices only hire for CBT. If you have bipolar disorder and you're only getting CBT, the research suggests you're getting the wrong treatment.

Manhattan Mental Health Counseling offers DBT, CBT, EMDR, psychodynamic, and more. 80+ therapists across 10+ modalities. Online in New York State.

Did you know the type of therapy matters as much as the therapist?
